title: All But Blind
content:
All but blind
        In his chambered hole
     Gropes for worms
        The four-clawed Mole.

     All but blind
        In the evening sky
     The hooded Bat
        Twirls softly by.

     All but blind
        In the burning day
     The Barn-Owl blunders
        On her way.

     And blind as are
        These three to me,
     So, blind to Some-one
        I must be.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter De La Mare, titled "All But Blind", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
